key_insight: Strain changes chemical ordering in NiPt nanoalloys in a structure-dependent way: truncated octahedra stay robust, while icosahedra redistribute Ni strongly under tension.
engineering_relevance: By combining substrate-like strain with particle shape, engineers can tune surface composition and segregation in supported alloy nanoparticles for catalysis and other functional nanomaterials.
method: Monte Carlo sampling of chemical ordering
Controlled tensile and compressive strain via an implicit anchored interface
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic simulation comparisons across NiPt nanoparticles with distinct motifs, showing little ordering change in truncated octahedra but pronounced strain-driven surface Ni enrichment in icosahedra.
watch_item: The results are simulation-based and use an implicit interface, so real supported nanoparticles may show additional effects from specific substrates, defects, or kinetics.
paper_title: Structure-Dependent Chemical Order Modification in Strained Alloy Nanoparticles
